entries.definition:
كتاب الفاء

فرك

(فري) الفاء والراء والحرف المعتلّ عُظْمُ البابِ قَطْعُ الشيء، ثم يفرَّع منه ما يقاربُه: من ذلك: فَرَيْتُ الشيء أفرِيه فرياً، وذلك قَطْعُكَه لإصلاحه. قال ابن
better or God, the One, the All-Powerful?
entries.definition_english: better or God, the One, the All-Powerful?

entries.definition:
f–r–y to cut, to chop, to tear up; to acquire riches; to fabricate,
to feign, to forge, to invent. Of this root, eight forms occur 60
times in the Qur’an: افْتَرَى ʾiftarā 49 times; يُفْتَرَى yuftarā twice;
افْتِرَاء ʾiftirāʾ twice; مُفْتَرًى muftarā twice; مُفْتَرَيَات muftarayāt once;
مُفْتَرٍ muftarī once; مُفْتَرُون muftarūn twice and فَرِيًّا fariyyan once.

افْتَرَى ʾiftarā [v. VIII, trans.] 1 to forge, to fabricate, to make
something up (11:35) أَمْ يَقُولُونَ افْتَرَاهُ قُلْ إِنِ افْتَرَيْتُهُ فَعَلَيَّ إِجْرَامِي maybe
they will say, ‘He has made it up’–say, ‘If I have made it up, upon
me falls my own crime’ 2 to commit an abomination (4:48) وَمَنْ
يُشْرِكْ بِاللَّهِ فَقَدِ افْتَرَى إِثْمًا عَظِيمًا but anyone who assigns partners to God
has concocted a tremendous sin 3 [with prep. عَلَى] to attribute
something to someone falsely (17:73) وَإِنْ كَادُوا لَيَفْتِنُونَكَ عَنِ الَّذِي أَوْحَيْنَا
إِلَيْكَ لِتَفْتَرِيَ عَلَيْنَا غَيْرَهُ [Prophet] the disbelievers planned to tempt you
away from what We revealed to you, so that you would attribute
to Us some other [revelation].

افْتِرَاء ʾiftirāʾ [v. n.] forgery, calumny, false representation,
false attribution (6:140) وَحَرَّمُوا مَا رَزَقَهُمُ اللَّهُ افْتِرَاءً عَلَى اللَّهِ and [they]
forbade that which God provided them, falsely attributing [all
this] to God.

مُفْتَرٍ muftarī [act. part.; pl. مُفْتَرُون muftarūn] inventor of
falsehood, forger, one who fabricates (11:50) إِنْ أَنْتُمْ إِلَّا مُفْتَرُونَ you
are nothing but fabricators.

مُفْتَرًى muftarā [pass. part.; fem. pl. مُفْتَرَيَات muftarayāt]
invented, fabricated without foundation, forged (28:36) قَالُوا مَا هَذَا
إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُفْتَرًى they said, ‘This is nothing but forged magic.’

فَرِيًّا fariyyan [quasi-act. part.] great, wonderful, amazing;
contrived, shocking, unprecedented (19:27) قَالُوا يَامَرْيَمُ لَقَدْ جِئْتِ شَيْئًا

708
